title: Clarkella nana
taxonomy: Clarkella nana is classified as a species within the genus Clarkella, which belongs to the family Gekkonidae, commonly known as the gecko family. This family is a part of the infraorder Gekkota, which is a group of lizards that also includes the families Phyllodactylidae and Pygopodidae. Gekkota is a subgroup of the infraorder Sauria, which comprises various species of lizards. The class Reptilia is the taxonomic rank above Sauria, and it includes all reptiles. The phylum Chordata is the highest taxonomic rank that includes Clarkella nana.

intro: Clarkella nana, also known as the Australian bush tomato, is a small, shrub-like plant native to the arid regions of Western Australia. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
